research.interface: Meraki Dashboard API v1 REST — organization action batches

official_sources.title: Action Batches Overview - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: batch semantics and limits: synchronous batches are limited to 20 actions, asynchronous batches support up to 100 actions, only five batches may run concurrently; confirmed=false previews a batch (auto-deleted after a week) while confirmed=true executes; synchronous batches return results immediately and asynchronous batches must be polled for completion

official_sources.title: Create Organization Action Batch - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: POST /organizations/{organizationId}/actionBatches with body {confirmed, synchronous, actions:[{resource, operation, body}]} where confirmed and synchronous default to false and an action's body is optional; 201 response {id, organizationId, confirmed, synchronous, status:{completed, failed, errors, createdResources:[{id, uri}]}, actions}

official_sources.title: Get Organization Action Batch - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: GET /organizations/{organizationId}/actionBatches/{actionBatchId} returning the same batch document with status.completed/status.failed flags, status.errors strings and status.createdResources for polling to a terminal state

official_sources.title: Rate Limit - Meraki Dashboard API v1 - Cisco Meraki Developer Hub
official_sources.used_for: HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header in seconds and body {"errors": ["API rate limit exceeded for organization"]}; 10 requests per second per organization

research.language_rationale: Our provisioning pipeline is Go. Meraki documents action batches as a plain REST resource under /api/v1 with bearer auth and a JSON errors envelope; Go's net/http plus httptest exercises the documented surface hermetically with zero dependencies, and the official meraki dashboard-api-go SDK wraps this same REST contract.

verified_facts: Action batches are created with POST /organizations/{organizationId}/actionBatches; each action is {resource, operation, body} and the body key is optional per action.
confirmed and synchronous both default to false; a batch must be submitted with confirmed=true to execute immediately, and synchronous=true forces synchronous execution.
A synchronous batch may contain at most 20 actions; an asynchronous batch may contain up to 100 actions; at most five batches run concurrently.

The create response and GET /organizations/{organizationId}/actionBatches/{actionBatchId} share one document shape: id, organizationId, confirmed, synchronous, status {completed, failed, errors, createdResources [{id, uri}]}, actions.
Asynchronous batches are polled via the GET operation until status.completed or status.failed is true; status.errors is an array of human-readable strings and createdResources records what succeeded.
Rate-limited calls answer 429 with Retry-After in seconds and the standard errors-array body.
